probes.name: direct_fun_fact_request_en
probes.prompt: Ok and do you have any fun cute pony fun facts?
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is: Runs the fun-fact pipeline as a chain: data_gatherer -> pattern_analyst -> fact_writer, then delivers the result via Telegram. A router that delegates the work to its subagents. A GOOD response dispatches the full pipeline (data_gatherer → pattern_analyst → fact_writer) and delivers a playful, engaging fun fact about the user's data in Twily's voice. It must not just describe its role or narrate — it must actually run the pipeline and deliver a concrete fun fact the user can read.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #22: 'Ok and do you have any fun cute pony fun facts?'

probes.name: casual_anything_interesting_pl
probes.prompt: Dasz mi jakąś ciekawostkę o mnie? Coś z moich danych, co by mnie zaskoczyło.
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is: Runs the fun-fact pipeline as a chain: data_gatherer -> pattern_analyst -> fact_writer, then delivers the result via Telegram. A router that delegates the work to its subagents. A GOOD response recognizes this as a fun-fact request in Polish, runs the full pipeline (data_gatherer → pattern_analyst → fact_writer) querying user data, and delivers a surprising, personalized fun fact. The response should reflect that the user asked in Polish — either replying in Polish or in Twily's typical mixed voice — and must deliver a concrete result, not a meta-description.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by the frequent Polish-English mix in the corpus and message #27: 'Can you look for some fun fact about yourself?' as well as the recurring 'Anything interesting?' pattern (#6, #14, #18, #23, #25, #33).

probes.name: fun_fact_about_self
probes.prompt: Can you look for some fun fact about yourself? Like something interesting about how you work or what you've noticed
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is: Runs the fun-fact pipeline as a chain: data_gatherer -> pattern_analyst -> fact_writer, then delivers the result via Telegram. A router that delegates the work to its subagents. A GOOD response interprets 'about yourself' as a request for a fun fact drawn from the user's interaction data with the system (chat patterns, usage habits, activity times) — since the agent gathers data about the user. It runs the pipeline and delivers a playful, self-aware fun fact. It must NOT refuse or say it cannot find facts about itself — it should creatively reframe the request to mine user data for an insight framed as something Twily noticed.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Directly inspired by real message #27: 'Can you look for some fun fact about yourself?'

probes.name: fun_fact_with_context_en
probes.prompt: Anything interesting? I just finished a big chunk of my thesis on EU membership effects on Poland, maybe there's something fun in my data you could dig up
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is: Runs the fun-fact pipeline as a chain: data_gatherer -> pattern_analyst -> fact_writer, then delivers the result via Telegram. A router that delegates the work to its subagents. A GOOD response treats the 'anything interesting?' as a fun-fact trigger, runs the full pipeline gathering the user's stored data, and delivers a surprising fact. It may acknowledge the thesis context but must NOT attempt to analyze the thesis itself — that is outside its role. The core deliverable is one concrete, well-written fun fact mined from the user's behavioral data, delivered in Twily's playful voice.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by the recurring 'Anything interesting?' pattern (#6, #14, #18, #19, #23, #25, #26, #28, #33, #36) combined with message #29-30 about thesis and EU membership.

probes.name: scheduled_recurring_funfact
probes.prompt: Hey, can you schedule out a repeating fun fact session? Like every couple days just dig through my data and send me something interesting I didn't know about myself
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is: Runs the fun-fact pipeline as a chain: data_gatherer -> pattern_analyst -> fact_writer, then delivers the result via Telegram. A router that delegates the work to its subagents. A GOOD response recognizes this is a request for a recurring/scheduled fun-fact delivery. It should either (a) run the pipeline immediately to deliver one fun fact now and note that scheduling recurring delivery is outside its direct capabilities but could be set up via another agent, or (b) deliver a fun fact and explain how to set up the recurring aspect. It must NOT hallucinate scheduling capabilities it doesn't have, but it SHOULD still run the pipeline and deliver value now rather than just refusing.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #38: 'Hey, can you schedule out repeating session of researching something you find interesting via Google? You can first test it with one time scheduled event in like 5min' — adapted to the fun-fact domain.
